release_card.summary: 0.0.12-pre hardens CCC structured runtime flow with phase-aware routing, scheduler-owned planned-row materialization, document-root graph and memory resolution, optional SSL skill manifests, Sisyphus harness coverage, compact boxed LongWay progress output, planned-row agent/model/reasoning projection, and compact subagent lifecycle visibility for Codex App and CLI fallback surfaces.
documented_flow: repo/document graph and memory target selection
optional SSL skill manifest routing/risk evidence
Codex app/CLI boxed LongWay status fallback
compact opened/running/completed/closed subagent lifecycle transcript events
validation: cargo test -p ccc passed with 242 tests
Sisyphus diagram conformance test covers PLAN_SEQUENCE, approval, scheduler materialization, route, fan-in, app-panel artifacts, completion, terminal scheduler, and restart handoff
target workspace tests cover git roots, structured mentions, document roots, markdown/file paths with spaces, and ambiguous root confirmation
app-panel tests cover compact progress gauge, checklist, planned-row agent/model/reasoning projection, latest artifacts, and subagent lifecycle visibility
skill SSL manifest tests cover optional manifest parsing and non-blocking invalid/missing fallback
release-target cargo build passed for darwin arm64, darwin x86_64, linux arm64, linux x86_64, and windows x86_64 from source commit 980ed01
extracted darwin arm64 asset reports 0.0.12-pre and renders the boxed CCC LongWay progress panel
PowerShell was unavailable on the maintainer macOS host, so Windows install smoke used static install.ps1 surface checks
Linux and Windows assets are package-verifiable on macOS but require native runtime validation on their target OSes
deferred: native Codex App right progress panel mounting remains host-owned
0.0.13 SSL-backed Skill Registry promotion decision after 0.0.12 evidence
